# Stepper Proof (Dual AVR + Web Serial + Physical Commander)
> Controle simultâneo e independente de dois motores de passo TB6600 via Web Serial API e interface física dedicada.

## Core Files
- [stepcontrol/stepcontrol.ino]: Firmware AVR (C++). Timer1 Dual-Channel Freerunning. Dual-input: USB Serial + SoftwareSerial (D10/D11). Protocolo H8P (01-1C).
- [stepcommander-v2/stepcommander-v2.ino]: Periférico de I/O V2 (Atmega328P). Não-bloqueante. Teclado 4x4 + LCD I2C + 10 macros EEPROM locais.
- [stepcommander/stepcommander.ino]: Periférico de I/O V1 (Atmega328P). Simples/Legacy.
- [public/app.js]: Main UI logic. Serial link, H8P parser, live telemetry, and jog scrubbers.
- [public/i18n.js]: Translation engine (EN/PT) with dynamic dictionary override system.
- [docs/INTEGRATION.md]: Technical API Reference. H8P protocol, response routing, and dual-interface architecture.
- [docs/STEPCOMMANDER.md]: Commander hardware setup, shortcuts, and LCD feedback reference.

## Key Concepts

### Dual-Interface Architecture
- **USB Serial (Web)**: Full H8P responses with complete parameters. Receives D0/C1 telemetry.
- **SoftwareSerial (Commander)**: Optimized short responses (code + ID only). No high-frequency telemetry.
- **Routing**: Firmware auto-detects command origin and routes responses accordingly.
- **Constraint**: SoftwareSerial blocks interrupts during TX (~1ms/byte), so high-frequency data is USB-only to prevent motor stutter.

### H8P Protocol (Hex 8-bit Protocol)
- **Architecture**: Pair-based `Key:Value` hex strings (ex: `10:1600`) separated by commas.
- **Commands**: `01` (RUN), `02` (STOP), `03` (Loop), `04` (Pause), `10-15` (Motor params), `16-17` (Driver EN), `18-1C` (EEPROM presets).
- **Safety**: Minimum interval clamp (50µs) and atomic hardware stops (`cli`/`sei`).
- **Memory**: 20 slots SRAM queue + 10 EEPROM presets.

### Dual-Channel Hardware Timing
- **M1**: Driven by `TIMER1_COMPA_vect` (OCR1A).
- **M2**: Driven by `TIMER1_COMPB_vect` (OCR1B).
- **Independence**: Zero-jitter execution due to independent compare registries.

### Response Routing
- **Broadcast (both ports)**: State changes — B0, B1, B2, B4, B5, B6, B7, B8, A0.
- **Origin-only**: Errors (E0-E4) and data responses (C0, B9, BA, BB, BC).
- **USB-only**: High-frequency telemetry — D0 (active line), C1 (queue count).

### Persistence & Customization
- **EEPROM (Firmware)**: 10 action slots (`18`, `19`, `1A`, `1B`, `1C`) for preset execution.
- **EEPROM (Commander)**: 10 local macro slots (32 chars each) for fast command dispatch.
- **LocalStorage (Web)**: Custom i18n overrides and alert visibility preferences.

## Operational Constraints
- **Serial Baud Rate**: Fixed at **9600 bps** (both USB and SoftwareSerial).
- **Motor Compatibility**: Pulse/Dir/Enable interface (NEMA 17/23/34 via TB6600).
- **Atomic Stop**: Command `02` kills all interrupts and clears all queues.
- **Pins Used**: D2-D7 (Motors), D10-D11 (SoftwareSerial), D0-D1 (USB Serial).
